skill.name: lipsync
skill.description: Lip-sync a face to a specific audio track on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes across ByteDance OmniHuman (audio-driven full-body avatar from a portrait + audio), Sync Labs sync v2 / Pro (state-of-the-art mouth sync onto a video), Kling lipsync (audio-to-video and text-to-video with synced speech), and Creatify lipsync. The skill picks the right endpoint for the user's actual intent — portrait still + audio (avatar-style), source video + audio (mouth-swap on existing footage), or generate-and-sync from a script. Triggers on "lip sync", "lipsync", "make this video speak", "match audio to mouth", "dub video", "sync lips to voice", "Sync Labs", "voiceover sync", or any explicit ask to drive a face's mouth from an audio track.
skill.summary: Routes lip-sync requests across RunComfy AI models for audio-driven video generation
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: All 97 static analysis findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is a markdown documentation file containing code examples, reference URLs, and model documentation. Shell backtick patterns reference runcomfy CLI usage inside markdown code blocks, not executable commands. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate documentation links to the RunComfy platform. Filesystem references describe CLI token storage paths. The skill acknowledges dual-use nature of lip-syncing technology with a consent warning. No code-level vulnerabilities found.

low_findings.title: All static findings are false positives in markdown documentation context
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick shell patterns, hardcoded URLs, and filesystem references in SKILL.md. All occurrences are inside markdown documentation, fenced code blocks, or reference links. None represent executable code, active network requests, or actual filesystem access. These are safe documentation patterns for a markdown-based skill file.

content.user_title: Route lip-sync videos across multiple AI models
content.value_statement: Creating lip-synced videos requires selecting the right AI model for each input type. This skill automatically routes your request to the best RunComfy model based on whether you have a portrait still, source video, or a script.
actual_capabilities: Routes lip-sync requests to the correct RunComfy model based on input type and quality tier
Supports portrait-to-avatar via OmniHuman, video mouth-swap via Sync Labs, and script-to-video via Kling
Generates proper runcomfy run CLI commands with correct model endpoints and JSON input
Provides guidance on model selection, quality tiers, and budget considerations
Documents common patterns like multi-language dubbing and UGC ad creation
limitations: Requires the runcomfy CLI to be installed and authenticated with RunComfy
Cannot generate audio or video content directly - relies on third-party RunComfy API endpoints
Output quality depends on source video clarity, audio quality, and duration matching
Does not verify legal consent or rights for the source media used in requests

use_cases.title: Create talking-head videos from a portrait photo
use_cases.description: Provide a portrait still and a voiceover audio file. The skill routes to OmniHuman to generate a video where the subject speaks naturally.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Dub existing video footage into new languages
use_cases.description: Supply a source video and a translated voiceover file. The skill routes to Sync Labs to replace the mouth motion with accurate lip sync for the new audio.

use_cases.title: Generate videos from a script with synced speech
use_cases.description: Provide a written script without an audio file. The skill routes to Kling Lipsync text-to-video to generate both speech audio and synced video.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and social media creators

prompt_templates.title: Basic lip sync from existing media
prompt_templates.prompt: Sync this video with this audio track using lip sync.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a source video and a voiceover audio file

prompt_templates.title: Talking-head avatar from portrait
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking-head video from this portrait photo and voiceover audio.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a single portrait image and wants a speaking avatar

prompt_templates.title: Script-to-video generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video from this script with synced speech. No audio file is available.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a text script but no pre-recorded audio

prompt_templates.title: Multi-language video dubbing
prompt_templates.prompt: Dub this brand video into Spanish, French, and German. Use the same source video with separate translated voiceover files for each language.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs the same video content adapted for multiple language markets

output_examples.input: Lip sync this video with my voiceover audio file.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies this as a source-video-plus-audio workflow. It selects Sync Labs sync v2 Pro as the recommended model and provides the runcomfy run command targeting the sync/sync/lipsync/v2/pro endpoint with the video and audio URLs in the JSON input.

output_examples.input: Turn this portrait photo into a talking avatar with this voice recording.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies this as a portrait-still-plus-audio workflow. It routes to OmniHuman (bytedance/omnihuman/api), provides the runcomfy run command, and includes tips about portrait framing and expected output quality.

output_examples.input: I have a script for a 30-second product ad but no audio file.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies this as a script-only workflow. It routes to Kling Lipsync text-to-video which generates speech audio from the script and syncs it to the resulting video in a single pass.

best_practices: Match audio length to video length for best sync quality and to avoid lip-sync drift
Use clean voiceover audio without background music to achieve the cleanest mouth sync results
Verify consent for both the person in the video and the voice in the audio track before processing
anti_patterns: Requesting lip-sync of real public figures without their explicit consent for the specific use case
Using poor quality audio with background noise or music, which degrades sync accuracy
Pairing significantly mismatched audio and video durations without trimming or extending media first

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need the runcomfy CLI installed (npm i -g @runcomfy/cli) and authenticated via runcomfy login or RUNCOMFY_TOKEN. You also need source media files such as a video or portrait image and an audio track.

faq.question: What AI models does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports OmniHuman, Sync Labs sync v2 and v2 Pro, Kling Lipsync (audio-to-video and text-to-video), Creatify Lipsync, Wan 2-7, and HappyHorse 1.0. The skill selects the best model based on your input type.

faq.question: Can I use this for real-time or live lip sync?
faq.answer: No. Processing is asynchronous through the RunComfy API. Results are available after the model finishes processing, which takes from seconds to minutes depending on the model and input size.

faq.question: What happens to the media files I provide?
faq.answer: Your media files are sent to the RunComfy API for model processing. The skill itself does not store, host, or redistribute your files. Refer to the RunComfy privacy policy for their data handling.

faq.question: Can I dub a video into multiple languages?
faq.answer: Yes. Use a single source video with separate translated voiceover audio files. Run each language as a separate request through Sync Labs, which preserves the original video and only updates the mouth motion.

faq.question: Which model gives the best quality?
faq.answer: Sync Labs sync v2 Pro offers the highest mouth fidelity for video-to-video lip sync. OmniHuman gives the best results for portrait-to-avatar workflows. Model choice depends on your input type and quality requirements.
